plan.title: Plan - Frontend/Backend Coverage 95 Orchestrated
plan.objective: Raise practical automated test coverage for both backend and frontend to at least 95 percent on all tracked metrics in this campaign, using realistic regression-oriented tests and orchestrated atomic subagent execution.
in_scope: Define measurable coverage scope, identify current gaps, and align command-level evidence strategy with memory and queue constraints.
Coverage baseline inventory with explicit target metrics and feasible command matrix.
out_of_scope: Do not run monolithic full-suite commands that are known to exceed local RAM limits. Do not add synthetic or brittle tests that only inflate numbers without validating user-facing behavior or core service contracts.

acceptance_criteria: Current backend/frontend coverage baselines are captured from runnable targeted commands
Checklist schema reflects both backend and frontend sections with command provenance
tasks.outcome: Captured backend and frontend baseline scoped metrics before test expansion. | Established refreshed checklist schema for dual-scope coverage tracking.

acceptance_criteria: Backend tracked coverage metrics meet or exceed 95 percent
New backend tests validate realistic success and failure behavior for previously under-covered logic
tasks.outcome: Added realistic backend tests for auth, notification, and error fallback paths. | Raised backend scoped metrics to >=95 across statements/branches/functions/lines.

acceptance_criteria: Frontend tracked coverage metrics meet or exceed 95 percent
New frontend tests validate realistic UI/runtime behavior, not only static rendering
tasks.outcome: Added deterministic frontend tests for playback reconciliation, API base URL resolution, queue insertion, auto-match guards, and recovery policy edge paths. | Raised frontend scoped metrics to >=95 across statements/branches/functions/lines.

acceptance_criteria: Checklist contains final coverage metrics for frontend and backend with timestamps and commands
Queue item completion evidence includes explicit verify-prefixed entries
tasks.outcome: Incremented and rewrote machine-readable checklist with baseline/final metrics, commands, artifacts, and aggregate pass state. | Recorded dual-scope campaign metadata and retained legacy snapshot context.

pre_spec_outline.purpose_goals: Raise practical automated test coverage for both backend and frontend to at least 95 percent on all tracked metrics in this campaign, using realistic regression-oriented tests and orchestrated atomic subagent execution.
pre_spec_outline.non_goals: Do not run monolithic full-suite commands that are known to exceed local RAM limits. Do not add synthetic or brittle tests that only inflate numbers without validating user-facing behavior or core service contracts.

spec_outline.summary: Establish a constrained, orchestrated coverage campaign that reaches and proves 95 percent minimum coverage for frontend and backend tracked surfaces while keeping tests meaningful and maintainable.

full_spec_outline.objective: Define measurable coverage scope, identify current gaps, and align command-level evidence strategy with memory and queue constraints.
full_spec_outline.deliverable: Coverage baseline inventory with explicit target metrics and feasible command matrix.
acceptance_criteria: Coverage sources and metrics are documented for frontend and backend
Outdated checklist scope is corrected and replaced with current campaign schema

refined_spec.summary: Execute through atomic subagent tasks: baseline measurement, targeted backend test lifts, targeted frontend test lifts, and final checklist synchronization with verification artifacts.

full_refined_spec.decision: Use worker/explorer subagents for all substantive discovery and implementation tasks, reserving orchestrator work for queue control, plan updates, integration review, and mandatory verification commands.
full_refined_spec.rationale: This follows repository orchestration policy and allows parallel, bounded slices of work while minimizing merge conflicts and preserving test quality through focused ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Each subagent is assigned a single atomic objective with file ownership boundaries
Each completed slice includes targeted verification output that can be traced back to checklist evidence

implementation_plan.summary: Drive iterative coverage waves with measurable checkpoints and keep machine-readable coverage checklist synchronized after each verified increment until both frontend and backend metrics satisfy the 95 percent floor.

acceptance_criteria: Current backend/frontend coverage baselines are captured from runnable targeted commands
Checklist schema reflects both backend and frontend sections with command provenance

delivered: Captured backend and frontend baseline scoped metrics before test expansion.
Established refreshed checklist schema for dual-scope coverage tracking.

acceptance_criteria: Backend tracked coverage metrics meet or exceed 95 percent
New backend tests validate realistic success and failure behavior for previously under-covered logic

delivered: Added realistic backend tests for auth, notification, and error fallback paths.
Raised backend scoped metrics to >=95 across statements/branches/functions/lines.

acceptance_criteria: Frontend tracked coverage metrics meet or exceed 95 percent
New frontend tests validate realistic UI/runtime behavior, not only static rendering

delivered: Added deterministic frontend tests for playback reconciliation, API base URL resolution, queue insertion, auto-match guards, and recovery policy edge paths.
Raised frontend scoped metrics to >=95 across statements/branches/functions/lines.

acceptance_criteria: Checklist contains final coverage metrics for frontend and backend with timestamps and commands
Queue item completion evidence includes explicit verify-prefixed entries

delivered: Incremented and rewrote machine-readable checklist with baseline/final metrics, commands, artifacts, and aggregate pass state.
Recorded dual-scope campaign metadata and retained legacy snapshot context.
